codeReview.summary: The commit adds useful focused primitive coverage for BiometricVault.lock() and useAgentStore.teardown(), but it does not implement or verify the hook-level behavior required by the validation assertions this feature claims.

issues.description: The feature claims VAL-VAULT-020 and VAL-VAULT-021, but useAutoLock still only calls session.lock() on background/inactive and still depends on AUTO_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS instead of tearing down the agent. There are also no hook tests under src/hooks proving teardown-on-background or a fresh NativeBiometricVault.getSecret call after auto-lock, so the claimed validation-contract assertions remain unfulfilled.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature metadata is out of sync with the validation contract: this feature is described as primitive pinning while its fulfills list claims hook-level assertions VAL-VAULT-020 and VAL-VAULT-021.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:1264-1282 says the next milestone wires useAutoLock, yet lists fulfills=[VAL-VAULT-020, VAL-VAULT-021]; validation-contract.md:1700-1744 defines those assertions as useAutoLock hook behavior/tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true even though the transcript skeleton does not show a red-first run for the newly added suites.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl:20 shows baseline test execution, then creation of the new test files, then the first scoped run already passing; the handoff file records skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Review failed: the commit is a useful primitive-test addition, but it does not implement or verify the useAutoLock behavior required for VAL-VAULT-020/021, so those assertions are still unmet.
